html:
Exclusive to:

Weeds are objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together and introduced in Return of Them. They interfere with the farming of Farm Plants, but they also have some benefits. Weeds can be removed with a Shovel or by burning them.

Contents

  • 1 Spawning
  • 2 Growth
  • 3 Forget-Me-Lots
  • 4 Fire Nettles
  • 5 Tillweed
  • 6 Spiny Bindweed
  • 7 Gallery
  • 8 Trivia

Spawning

There are 4 type of Weeds: Forget-Me-Lots, Fire Nettles, Tillweed and Spiny Bindweed. A planted regular Seed has a 20% chance to grow into a weed, with a 77% chance to become a Forget-Me-Lots and a 7.7% chance to become a Fire Nettle, a Tillweed, or a Spiny Bindweed.

At the beginning of Spring, each Farm Soil Tile has a 15% chance to grow a weed. The weed will appear randomly in the first quarter of the season. The same process happens in Autumn with a 5% chance.

At the final stage, after 6 to 8 days (3 to 5 for Forget-Me-Lots), the weed will spawn a new weed of the same type within a radius from 1.5 to 10 units with a preference for farm soil. Then, every 12 to 14 days (6 to 8 days for Forget-Me-Lots) the weed attempts a new spread.

A Fruit Fly plants new weeds on all unused farm soil in its sight; thus, by strategically spawning the Lord of the Fruit Flies boss in a vacant area, it can be aggroed over a neighboring pre-tilled farm plot to grow a plentiful amount of weeds immediately. The weeds can then be harvested, dug up, or burnt and re-tilled for a new harvest.

Growth

Fire Nettles, Tillweed and Spiny Bindweed have 3 growing stages. Forget-Me-Lots have an additional stage called Bolting.

Growth time in daylight (day)
Plant
Seeds 0.75 - 1
Small 1.2 - 1.8 0.6 - 0.9
Medium 0.8 - 1.2 0.6 - 0.9
Full Ever 0.8 - 1.2
Bolting - Ever

At the full stage, Fire Nettles, Tillweed, and Forget-Me-Lots can be harvested. If so, they return to the beginning of the small stage and grow again. If Forget-Me-Lots is left to grow into bolting stage, it can no longer be harvested.

Growth times are not affected by the season or Nutrients. Weeds consume 2 of each nutrient at each stage of their growth and produce no nutrients in return.

Plant Name Product Nutrient Drink Rate
Growth
Formula 		Compost Manure
Forget-Me-Lots -2 -2 -2 0.75%
Fire Nettles -2 -2 -2 2%
Tillweed -2 -2 -2 2%
Spiny Bindweed - -2 -2 -2 2%

Forget-Me-Lots

“With their pleasant, relaxing scent, it's often difficult to remember they're weeds.”

– W

When a Forget-Me-Lots is dug up, there is a chance for a new Forget-Me-Lots to grow in a nearby tile after 2 to 5 days. The chance depends on the number of other Forget-Me-Lots present; there is a 75% chance of it respawning if the plant is alone, 50% if there is another one within a 3 tile radius, 25% if there are two others, and 0% for 3 or more. Burning this weed will prevent regrowth. However, burning one crop tends to burn them all and possibly the base as well, so don't try that without adequate ice flingomatic coverage or similar fire suppression.

Fire Nettles

“These nettles spread through a garden like wildfire, and carry a burning toxin.”

– W

A player that comes into contact with Fire Nettles receives 3 damage and their perceived temperature is increased by 60° for 1 minute, making the player subject to Overheating. Wormwood is immune to this effect. The plant does not trigger its effect while wet. They can be weeded out using the Shovel.

Tillweed

“An irritatingly stubborn weed that requires garden tools to remove entirely.”

– W

Tillweed spawns a new Garden Detritus every 0.75 to 1 days. There can be up to 5 Garden Detritus within a radius of 5 around the plant.

Spiny Bindweed

“An aggressive, invasive species that will invade your own space when provoked.”

– W

The Spiny Bindweed protects all plants within a 2.5 tile radius. When a player harvests a protected plant, Binding Roots will trap the player. Binding Roots deal 10 damage and will trap the player until destroyed. Binding Roots have 40 health. Wormwood is immune to this effect.

Gallery

  • Unused Spiny Bindweed Spike

Trivia

  • The Weeds were introduced in the Reap What You Sow update.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cỏ dại là vật thể độc quyền trong Don't Starve Together và được giới thiệu trong Return of Them. Chúng can thiệp vào việc trồng Cây Nông nghiệp, nhưng chúng cũng có một số lợi ích. Cỏ dại có thể được loại bỏ bằng Xẻng hoặc bằng cách đốt chúng.
title: Weeds
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Clear Mobile Infobox}} '''Weeds''' are objects exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' and introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. They interfere with the farming of [[Farm Plant]]s, but they also have some benefits. Weeds can be removed with a [[Shovel]] or by burning them. __TOC__ ==Spawning== There are 4 type of Weeds: '''Forget-Me-Lots''', '''Fire Nettles''', '''Tillweed''' and '''Spiny Bindweed'''. A planted regular [[Seeds|Seed]] has a 20% chance to grow into a weed, with a 77% chance to become a Forget-Me-Lots and a 7.7% chance to become a Fire Nettle, a Tillweed, or a Spiny Bindweed. At the beginning of [[Spring]], each [[Farm Soil|Farm Soil Tile]] has a 15% chance to grow a weed. The weed will appear randomly in the first quarter of the season. The same process happens in [[Autumn]] with a 5% chance. At the final stage, after 6 to 8 days (3 to 5 for Forget-Me-Lots), the weed will spawn a new weed of the same type within a radius from 1.5 to 10 units with a preference for farm soil. Then, every 12 to 14 days (6 to 8 days for Forget-Me-Lots) the weed attempts a new spread. A [[Fruit Fly]] plants new weeds on all unused farm soil in its sight; thus, by strategically spawning the [[Lord of the Fruit Flies]] boss in a vacant area, it can be aggroed over a neighboring pre-tilled farm plot to grow a plentiful amount of weeds immediately. The weeds can then be harvested, dug up, or burnt and re-tilled for a new harvest. ==Growth== Fire Nettles, Tillweed and Spiny Bindweed have 3 growing stages. Forget-Me-Lots have an additional stage called Bolting. {|class = "wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ Growth time in daylight (day) ! Plant ! {{pic|x32|Spiny Bindweed}} ! {{pic|x32|Tillweed}} ! {{pic|x32|Fire Nettles}} ! {{pic|x32|Forget-Me-Lots Plant}} |- | Seeds | colspan=4 | 0.75 - 1 |- | Small | colspan=3 | 1.2 - 1.8 | 0.6 - 0.9 |- | Medium | colspan=3 | 0.8 - 1.2 | 0.6 - 0.9 |- | Full | colspan = 3 | Ever | 0.8 - 1.2 |- | Bolting | colspan=3 | - | Ever |} At the full stage, Fire Nettles, Tillweed, and Forget-Me-Lots can be harvested. If so, they return to the beginning of the small stage and grow again. If Forget-Me-Lots is left to grow into bolting stage, it can no longer be harvested. Growth times are not affected by the season or [[Nutrient]]s. Weeds consume 2 of each nutrient at each stage of their growth and produce no nutrients in return. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" ! rowspan=2 class=unsortable | Plant ! rowspan=2 | Name ! rowspan=2 | Product ! colspan=3 | [[Nutrient]] ! rowspan=2 | Drink Rate |- ! rowspan=1 | Growth
Formula ! rowspan=1 | Compost ! rowspan=1 | Manure |- | {{pic|x32|Forget-Me-Lots Plant}} | [[#Forget-Me-Lots|Forget-Me-Lots]] | {{pic|32|Forget-Me-Lots}} | -2 | -2 | -2 | 0.75% |- | {{pic|x32|Fire Nettles}} | [[#Fire Nettles|Fire Nettles]] | {{pic|32|Fire Nettle Fronds}} | -2 | -2 | -2 | 2% |- | {{pic|x32|Tillweed}} | [[#Tillweed|Tillweed]] | {{pic|32|Tillweeds}} | -2 | -2 | -2 | 2% |- | {{pic|x32|Spiny Bindweed}} | [[#Spiny Bindweed|Spiny Bindweed]] | - | -2 | -2 | -2 | 2% |} ==Forget-Me-Lots== {{Plant Infobox |title1 = Forget-Me-Lots |image2 = Forget-Me-Lots Full.png|Weed Weedreg1.png|Plant Registry |small = Forget-Me-Lots Small.png |med = Forget-Me-Lots Med.png |bolting = Forget-Me-Lots Bolting.png |picked = Forget-Me-Lots Picked.png |tool = Can be [[Tools#Digging_Tools|Dug]] {{pic|24|Shovel}} |canBeBurned = 1 |resources = {{pic32|Forget-Me-Lots}} |perk = Spread rapidly |spawnCode = "weed_forgetmelots" |water_consumption = low |growth_formula = -1 |compost = -1 |manure = -1 }} {{Quote|With their pleasant, relaxing scent, it's often difficult to remember they're weeds.|from=W}} When a '''Forget-Me-Lots''' is dug up, there is a chance for a new Forget-Me-Lots to grow in a nearby tile after 2 to 5 days. The chance depends on the number of other Forget-Me-Lots present; there is a 75% chance of it respawning if the plant is alone, 50% if there is another one within a 3 tile radius, 25% if there are two others, and 0% for 3 or more. Burning this weed will prevent regrowth. However, burning one crop tends to burn them all and possibly the base as well, so don't try that without adequate ice flingomatic coverage or similar fire suppression. {{Clear}} ==Fire Nettles== {{Plant Infobox |title1 = Fire Nettles |image2 = Fire Nettles Full.png|Weed Nettlesreg1.png|Plant Registry |small = Fire Nettles Small.png |med = Fire Nettles Med.png |picked = Fire Nettles Picked.png |water_consumption = med |growth_formula = -1 |compost = -1 |manure = -1 |tool = Can be [[Tools#Digging_Tools|Dug]] {{pic|24|Shovel}} |canBeBurned = 1 |resources = {{pic32|Fire Nettle Fronds}} |perk = Overheat players |spawnCode = "weed_firenettle" }} {{Quote|These nettles spread through a garden like wildfire, and carry a burning toxin.|from=W}} A player that comes into contact with '''Fire Nettles''' receives 3 damage and their perceived temperature is increased by 60° for 1 minute, making the player subject to [[Overheating]]. [[Wormwood]] is immune to this effect. The plant does not trigger its effect while wet. They can be weeded out using the Shovel. {{Clear}} ==Tillweed== {{Plant Infobox |title1 = Tillweed |image2 = Tillweed Full.png|Weed Tillweedreg1.png|Plant Registry |small = Tillweed Small.png |med = Tillweed Med.png |picked = Tillweed Picked.png |water_consumption = med |growth_formula = -1 |compost = -1 |manure = -1 |tool = Can be [[Tools#Digging_Tools|Dug]] {{pic|24|Shovel}} |canBeBurned = 1 |resources = {{pic32|Tillweeds}} |perk = Spawn [[Garden Detritus]] |spawnCode = "weed_tillweed" }} {{Quote|An irritatingly stubborn weed that requires garden tools to remove entirely.|from=W}} '''Tillweed''' spawns a new [[Garden Detritus]] every 0.75 to 1 days. There can be up to 5 Garden Detritus within a radius of 5 around the plant. {{Clear}} ==Spiny Bindweed== {{Plant Infobox |title1 = Spiny Bindweed |image2 = Spiny Bindweed Full.png|Weed Spiny Bindweed reg1.png|Plant Registry |small = Spiny Bindweed Small.png |med = Spiny Bindweed Med.png |tool = Can be [[Tools#Digging_Tools|Dug]] {{pic|24|Shovel}} |canBeBurned = 1 |perk = Defend other plant |spawnCode = "weed_ivy" |water_consumption = med |growth_formula = -1 |compost = -1 |manure = -1 }} {{Object Infobox |name = Binding Root |image = Binding Root.png |damage = 10 |health = 40 }} {{Quote|An aggressive, invasive species that will invade your own space when provoked.|from=W}} The '''Spiny Bindweed''' protects all plants within a 2.5 tile radius. When a player harvests a protected plant, '''Binding Roots''' will trap the player. Binding Roots deal 10 damage and will trap the player until destroyed. Binding Roots have 40 health. [[Wormwood]] is immune to this effect. {{Clear}} =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Unused Spiny Bindweed Spike.png|Unused Spiny Bindweed Spike == {{Pic|32|Placeholder}}Trivia == * The Weeds were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Reap What You Sow|Reap What You Sow]]'' update. {{Naturally Spawning Objects}}
